objective: Objectif de stratégie : "Mettre en œuvre une variante d'adaptation de régime (trend vs range) combinant l'utilisation de filtres anti-faux-signaux pour améliorer la qualité des signaux et un écart entre les valeurs du ROC, ainsi qu'une confirmation contrarienne partielle dans le contexte d'un marché à effervescence. L'objectif est de génrer des profits en capitalisant sur les changements de tendance tout en minimisant les pertes en cas de consolidation ou de retour à la tendance, et avec un risque management basé sur le stop loss et le take profit.

iterations.hypothesis: Implementing an adaptive trend-range filter based on ATR and confirmation trading strategy reduces the number of fake signals in trending markets and optimizes trades for range bound conditions.

pre_reflection.text:
[Pre-reflection] This strategy is based on Bollinger, ATR and RSI indicators. It combines an adaptive trend filter with a range filter, uses anti-fake signals to improve the quality of signals, and employs confirmation contrarian trading in volatile markets. The results are not yet available.
[Backup plan] 1) Potential weaknesses may include overfitting or lack of robustness due to excessive use of indicators. To address this issue, more diverse data sets could be considered for testing. 2) If the strategy underperforms (negative return), adjustments might involve changing the threshold values of Bollinger bands and ATR indicator, or adding other filters like MACD, etc. 3) Modifying parameters such as RSI overbought/oversold thresholds, increasing stop loss distance from current price level could improve risk management. If all else fails, using a different approach based on machine learning algorithms might be considered.

iterations.hypothesis: Combining trend and range trading with ATR based risk management and confirmation for both trends and ranges.

pre_reflection.text: [Pre-reflection] The proposed strategy uses Bollinger Bands, Average True Range (ATR) and Relative Strength Index (RSI) for trend identification. It combines trend trading with rangebound strategies using ATR based risk management and confirmation signals. The strategy also employs stop loss orders to limit potential losses and take profit orders to lock in profits. The use of different filters to identify false signals is a strength, but it may be susceptible to slippage due to market conditions or system limitations. If results are poor (negative return), testing more robust filtering strategies could be considered. If the strategy performs poorly (low Sharpe ratio), adjusting stop loss and take profit orders based on trade size and volatility levels might improve performance. In case of failure, considering other trend following strategies that use different indicators may provide an alternative approach.

iterations.hypothesis: Using ATR to filter out false signals and a comparison of RSI & Donchian bands for trend confirmation in trending markets.
